text:
STARKVILLE â Five former Mississippi State athletes and coaches have won Olympic medals and that number could rise by the time 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris have concluded.

Mississippi State has its best chance to add more Olympic medalists with nine former athletes and coaches competing in Paris, the most ever in the schoolâs history. Hereâs a breakdown of the nine former Mississippi State athletes competing in Paris:

Catalina Perez (Colombia) and Ilana Izquierdo (Colombia) are the first-ever Bulldogs to compete in Olympic soccer. Izquierdo also becomes MSUâs 11th athlete to compete in the Olympics while an active athlete for MSU (Note: Columbiaâs womenâ team lost to France 3-2 on Thursday).

Womenâs Soccer:Colombia vs. New Zealand, 10 a.m. Sunday, Peacock

Nuno Borges (Portugal) is the first Bulldog ever to compete in menâs tennis and he does less than a week after defeating Rafael Nadal in straight sets on a clay court.

Menâs Singles:Borges vs. Navone (Argentina), Saturday, Peacock

Menâs Doubles:Portugal vs. Greece, Saturday, Peacock

Curtis Thompson (USA) is the only American-MSU athlete to compete in Paris this summer. This will be the second Olympic games appearance for MSUâs first-ever NCAA Champion in menâs javelin. Heâll be joined by Anderson Peters (Grenada) in the javelin competition.

Menâs Javelin:Qualifying Heats, 3:20 a.m. August 6

Marco Arop (Canada) and Navasky Anderson (Jamaica) will compete in the 800-meter run. This will be Aropâs second-straight Olympics appearance and Anderson will be the first Jamaican man to race in the 800M at the Olympics in 16 years.

Menâs 800M:First Round, 4:55 a.m. August 7

Lee Eppie (Botswana) and his 4x400-meter relay team hold the fastest time in the world by a national team this season. If they keep that streak going in Paris, Eppie could be one of four men to win Botswanaâs first-ever Olympic medal.

Menâs 4x400M Relay:First Round, 4:05 a.m. August 9

No games scheduled.

No games played.

description: Your daily briefing on what's going on with Mississippi State Athletics
